sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal; Arabic غدر to desert perfidiously; III. to desert.

Niphal נֶעְדַּר-

(1) to be left behind, to remain, 2Sa 17:22.

(2) to be wanting, lacking, 1Sa 30:19 Isa 40:26 59:15 .

Piel עִדֵּר to suffer anything to be wanting, 1Ki 5:7.

[This and the following are blended in Thes.]

II.

עדר

-

(1) to set in order, to arrange, to dispose, as an army in battle array, 1Ch 12:38. With the word for battle omitted, verse 33 1Ch 12:33. Hence עֵדֶר.

(2) From the Chaldee usage, to weed, Isa 5:6 7:25 (Syriae ܥܶܕܪܳܐ a plough); whence מַעְדֵּר a hoe. Hence-
